questions.text: I feel uneasy making decisions that affect others without their explicit buy-in.
I value directness in communication above all else.
I feel that I have a natural shock absorber for emotional impact.
I believe that bold strokes and big moves matter more than getting every detail right.
I find that I often forget my own needs because I am so focused on meeting the needs of those around me.
I hold the view that taking care of myself first puts me in the best position to live well.
I mix up different types of tasks throughout the day to keep things fresh.
I hold the view that a clear plan reduces wasted effort and increases impact.
I tend to be surprised by relationship dynamics that others saw developing all along.
I feel that the moral demand of suffering applies with equal force whether the person is present or distant.
I notice that my emotional reactions are often more intense than what the situation calls for.
I believe that a strong focus on personal achievement leads to a well-lived life.
I hold the view that remaining emotionally even during a crisis demonstrates a kind of competence that serves everyone better.
I believe my constitutional calmness is an asset in uncertain situations.
I have been told that I have a commanding presence.
I hold the view that accountability should be distributed across the team.
I notice when a compliment is designed to establish dominance rather than express admiration.
I think that rational engagement with suffering, while less dramatic than emotional engagement, tends to produce results that are more lasting and more just.
